items.title: Alibaba previewed a 2.4T Qwen model
items.oneliner: Alibaba previewed a 2.4T open-weight Qwen. Frontier-scale open models are no longer a Western-only game.

items.title: Xiaomi scaled robotics with web-scale data
items.oneliner: Xiaomi trained Robotics-1 on 100,000 hours plus 7,200 robot hours. Robotics is finally borrowing the LLM playbook.

items.title: A 1-bit browser LLM is usable
items.oneliner: Hugging Face showed a 1-bit WebGPU LLM in-browser. On-device inference keeps getting cheaper and harder to ignore.

items.title: AI found a cheap WordPress RCE
items.oneliner: A researcher says GPT5.6 found a WordPress RCE for $25. Vulnerability discovery is getting dramatically cheaper.

items.title: snac2 exposed an unauthenticated DoS flaw
items.oneliner: A fuzzing pass found unauthenticated DoS in snac2. Small federated servers still carry very real internet-facing risk.

items.title: The EU may widen biometric data sharing
items.oneliner: A leaked EU-US draft would expand biometric data sharing for visa-free travel. Privacy concessions now follow infrastructure dependence.

items.title: AI data centers are colliding with land rights
items.oneliner: With 3,000 US data centers running and 1,500 coming, AI's power appetite is becoming a legal and political fight.

items.title: Airbus is moving off AWS
items.oneliner: Airbus is shifting 900 apps from AWS to Scaleway. Sovereignty is becoming a board-level cloud architecture requirement.

items.title: AI buyers are shifting to outcome pricing
items.oneliner: OpenAI's CFO pushed useful intelligence per dollar, while builders keep burning tokens. Cost discipline is replacing API-era optimism.

items.title: LoRA tuning now has a speed leaderboard
items.oneliner: LoRA Speedrun turns fine-tuning into a wall-clock benchmark. Training efficiency is becoming a public competitive metric.
